title: Decaloba lunata
intro: Decaloba lunata, also known as the moonflower, is a species of flowering plant in the family Malpighiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Decaloba lunata include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which have been shown to possess antioxidant and immunomodulatory activities. Decaloba lunata is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also used in traditional medicine to make remedies for fever, wounds, and skin conditions.
